name: Thomson Reuters Elite 3E Legal Practice Management API
what_it_does: Thomson Reuters Elite 3E legal practice management REST API for law firm billing, matter management, time entry, financial reporting, and business intelligence. Enables AI agents to manage matter and client setup for automated client onboarding workflow, handle time entry and billing narrative capture for automated attorney time recording, access prebilling and invoice generation for billing cycle automation, retrieve accounts receivable and collections management for law firm cash flow automation, manage trust accounting and IOLTA compliance for fiduciary account automation, handle conflict of interest check retrieval for new matter intake automation, access financial reporting and profitability analysis for law firm business intelligence, retrieve realization and utilization metrics for attorney performance analytics, manage budget vs actual tracking for matter-level financial control, and integrate Elite 3E with document management (iManage, NetDocuments), e-billing platforms (Tymetrix, Passport), and CRM for full legal business automation.
use_cases: Automating time capture from attorney productivity agents
Processing invoices from billing automation agents
Tracking matter budgets from financial control agents
Managing AR collections from law firm finance agents
not_for: Small law firms without enterprise volume (use Clio or PracticePanther)
Legal e-discovery and document review (use Relativity or Everlaw)
Court filing and case management for courts (use Tyler Technologies)
best_when: An Am Law 200 or large regional law firm wanting AI agents to automate billing, matter management, time capture, trust accounting, conflict checking, and financial reporting with integration into the Elite 3E financial system of record.
avoid_when: IOLTA TRUST ACCOUNTING COMPLIANCE MANDATORY: Automated trust account transactions via Elite 3E must comply with state bar IOLTA rules (separate trust accounting, monthly reconciliation, five-year recordkeeping); automated trust fund disbursement without attorney approval creates state bar disciplinary and malpractice exposure. TIME ENTRY ATTORNEY CERTIFICATION: Automated time entry creation from AI agents must be reviewed and certified by the billing attorney before invoice generation; automated billing of AI-generated time entries without attorney certification creates ABA Model Rule 1.5 fee reasonableness and professional responsibility risk. CONFLICT CHECK AUTOMATION AND LATERAL HIRE LAG: Automated conflict check via Elite 3E is bounded by client and matter data loaded at time of check; lateral attorney hire conflict data may not be fully loaded; automated conflict clearance for laterals without supplemental conflict search creates malpractice exposure. ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE IN BILLING NARRATIVES: Automated billing narrative generation must not include privileged communications or work product detail; automated narratives with excessive client communication detail may waive privilege in fee dispute litigation.

auth.notes: Thomson Reuters Elite 3E uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. REST API with XML/JSON. New York City HQ (Thomson Reuters Corporation, NYSE: TRI / TSX: TRI). Thomson Reuters $7B+ annual revenue. Elite 3E is the flagship enterprise legal practice management platform for Am Law 200 firms. Originally Elite Information Group; acquired by Thomson Reuters 2010. Competes with Aderant Expert for Am Law enterprise legal practice management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise annual subscription with professional services implementation
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise annual subscription by attorney and staff user count; significant implementation professional services; cloud-hosted and on-premise
pricing.notes: New York NY. Thomson Reuters NYSE/TSX:TRI. $7B+ revenue. Elite 3E flagship legal ERP. Am Law 200 target market. Enterprise annual subscription. Multi-year implementation typical.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; billing and financial API throttled for data integrity

known_agent_gotchas: NO WEBHOOKS — BILLING CYCLE REQUIRES POLLING: Elite 3E does not support native webhooks; billing cycle completion, time entry posting, and invoice approval events require scheduled polling; automated billing workflow must implement polling-based event detection

IOLTA TRUST ACCOUNTING ATTORNEY APPROVAL MANDATORY: Automated trust fund disbursement or transfer via Elite 3E API must require attorney approval before execution; automated IOLTA transactions without attorney authorization create state bar disciplinary exposure and potential misappropriation of client funds

TIME ENTRY AI GENERATION REQUIRES ATTORNEY CERTIFICATION: AI-generated time entries created via Elite 3E API must be reviewed and certified by the responsible billing attorney before prebill or invoice; automated billing of AI-generated entries without certification creates ABA Model Rule 1.5 fee dispute and professional responsibility risk

Conflict check data currency for lateral hires — automated conflict check via Elite 3E relies on client and adverse party data loaded at time of check; lateral attorney hire conflict data may take days to load completely; supplement automated Elite 3E conflict check with manual search for lateral hire new matter intake

XML legacy API format for some endpoints — Elite 3E older API endpoints return XML format rather than JSON; automated agents must handle both XML and JSON response parsing depending on API version and endpoint; newer 3E API versions migrate to JSON

On-premise deployment network dependency — Elite 3E on-premise deployments require agent network access to law firm-hosted Elite servers; cloud AI agents need secure tunnel or VPN to on-premise Elite 3E; cloud-hosted Elite 3E available but requires separate cloud deployment
agent_readiness.security_notes: Legal practice management. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2/API key. US/EU/UK/AU. Attorney billing, client matter, and trust account data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Legal practice management. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2/API key. US/EU/UK/AU. Attorney billing and client matter data.
